sourceRef.citation: Kourovka Notebook, Problem 18.90 (Khukhro & Mazurov eds., ed.18)

statement.latex: Prove that the group$Y(m,n) = a_{1},a_{2}, ,a_{m} a_{k}^{n} =1 (1 k m) ( a_{k}^{i}a_{l}^{i}) ^{2} =e (1 k<l m 1 i {2})$is finite for every pair$( m,n)$. Some known cases are (where$C_n$denotes a cyclic group of order$n$):$Y(m,2)=C_{2}^{m}$;$Y(m;3)={A}_{m+2}$(presentation by Carmichael);$Y(m;4)$has order$2^{ {2}}$, nilpotency class$3$and exponent$4$; \$Y(2,n)$is the natural extension of the augment...

reiAssessment.solveRationale: Group theory I_INFINITE_SEARCH; Rei not specialized in group theory (yet)
